description: Letter from a certain Saʿadya to the Nagid Shemuʾel b. Ḥananya. Conveying information about a legal case. The case has to do with a warehouse of fiber tow (mushāq), likely from flax processing, belonging to the wife of al-Kohen known as Ibn al-Lādhiqī. Four men apparently opened the warehouse at the order of the government (l. 5). The four men are Abū l-Ḥasan the cantor known as Ibn מכור; Yaʿaqov and Khulayf the sons of Yosef al-Ḥazzan; and Khalaf b. Hiba known as al-Ṭunnāmilī. The document also mentions R. Yiṣḥaq; Abū l-Maʿālī; checking the court records (al-shimmush); Barakāt known as Ibn ʿĀdī. There are also other commodities involved, including codices (maṣāḥif). All this information was probably needed for an inheritance case. (Information in part from Goitein's index card.)
description_he: מכתב מסעדיה מסוים לנגיד שמואל ב' חנניה, עם מידע על תיק משפטי הקשור למחסן סיב פשתן השייך לאשת אל-כהן הנודע כאבן אל-לאד'קי. ארבעה אנשים פתחו את המחסן בהוראת הממשל.
